meta.description: ML system design interview questions for MLE roles at Meta

steps: Problem formulation (clarify objective, define ML task type)
Metrics (offline: AUC, precision/recall, NDCG; online: CTR, engagement, revenue)
Training pipeline (infrastructure, distributed training, hyperparameter tuning)
Serving and inference (latency, throughput, batch vs real-time)
Evaluation and experimentation (A/B testing, guardrail metrics)
Monitoring and iteration (data drift, model staleness, feedback loops)

questions.title: Design a News Feed Ranking System
questions.description: Design the ML system that ranks posts in a user's news feed to maximize engagement.
key_discussion_points: Multi-objective optimization (likes, comments, shares, time spent, negative feedback)
Feature engineering (user features, post features, context, social graph)
Training data: implicit vs explicit signals

expected_depth_by_level.E4: Core ranking formulation, basic features, logistic regression → neural network progression, offline metrics
expected_depth_by_level.E5: Multi-task learning, real-time features, position debiasing, online experimentation, fairness considerations

questions.title: Design an Ads Click-Through Rate Prediction System
questions.description: Design the ML system that predicts whether a user will click on an ad.
key_discussion_points: Feature engineering (user profile, ad features, context, historical interactions)
Model: logistic regression → GBDT → deep learning (DCN, DeepFM)
Training: large-scale, daily retraining, online learning

expected_depth_by_level.E4: Core CTR formulation, feature categories, model progression, basic serving
expected_depth_by_level.E5: Calibration importance, online learning, cold start solutions, privacy-preserving ML
expected_depth_by_level.E6: Full auction integration, privacy at scale, model efficiency research, org-wide ML platform

questions.title: Design a Content Recommendation System
questions.description: Design an ML system that recommends videos/reels/content to users.
key_discussion_points: Candidate generation: collaborative filtering, content-based, two-tower models
Cold start problem (new users, new content)

expected_depth_by_level.E4: Two-stage architecture, basic collaborative filtering, content features, offline evaluation
expected_depth_by_level.E5: Two-tower embeddings, ANN infrastructure, diversity injection, feedback loop mitigation, online experimentation

questions.title: Design a Harmful Content Detection System
questions.description: Design an ML system that detects harmful content (hate speech, violence, misinformation) across text, images, and video.
key_discussion_points: Taxonomy of violations (hate speech, violence, nudity, misinformation)
Labeling: human review pipelines, active learning
Model: BERT/transformer for text, CNN/ViT for images, multi-modal fusion
Precision vs recall trade-offs (false positive = censorship, false negative = harm)
Scaling: billions of posts per day

expected_depth_by_level.E4: Classification formulation, text model choice, precision/recall trade-off, basic pipeline
expected_depth_by_level.E5: Multi-modal fusion, adversarial robustness, active learning, human review optimization, policy iteration

questions.title: Design a People You May Know System
questions.description: Design the ML system behind friend/connection recommendations.
key_discussion_points: Feature engineering: profile similarity, interaction history, location
Model: gradient boosted trees → graph neural networks
Evaluation: friend request sent, accepted, long-term engagement

expected_depth_by_level.E4: Graph features, mutual friends baseline, classification model, basic evaluation
expected_depth_by_level.E5: Graph neural networks, privacy-preserving features, creepiness filtering, A/B testing methodology

questions.title: Design a Search Ranking System
questions.description: Design the ML system that ranks search results for Facebook/Instagram search.
key_discussion_points: Multi-vertical search (people, posts, groups, pages, marketplace)

expected_depth_by_level.E5: Query understanding pipeline, multi-stage retrieval, LTR models, evaluation methodology
expected_depth_by_level.E6: Cross-vertical unified ranking, embedding retrieval infrastructure, real-time indexing, platform-level search ML

questions.title: Design a Fraud Detection System
questions.description: Design an ML system that detects fraudulent accounts and activities on the platform.
key_discussion_points: Feature engineering: behavioral patterns, network analysis, device fingerprinting

expected_depth_by_level.E4: Classification formulation, class imbalance handling, basic features, evaluation metrics
expected_depth_by_level.E5: Graph-based detection, adversarial robustness, real-time scoring, feedback loop management

questions.title: Design an ML System for Estimated Time of Arrival
questions.description: Design an ML system that predicts delivery or travel time (applicable to Marketplace delivery, ride-sharing).
key_discussion_points: Features: distance, route, traffic patterns, time of day, weather
Model: gradient boosted trees → sequence models for routes

expected_depth_by_level.E4: Regression formulation, core features, model choice, evaluation metrics
expected_depth_by_level.E5: Spatial-temporal modeling, uncertainty quantification, real-time updates, graph-based route modeling

questions.title: Design a Video Understanding System
questions.description: Design an ML system that understands video content for recommendations, search, and safety.
key_discussion_points: Multi-modal: visual frames, audio, text (captions, OCR)
Video feature extraction (3D CNNs, Video Transformers)
Multiple downstream tasks: classification, search, recommendation

expected_depth_by_level.E5: Multi-modal architecture, efficient frame sampling, embedding pipeline, downstream task adaptation
expected_depth_by_level.E6: Foundation model approach, cross-modal pre-training, infrastructure for billion-scale, model efficiency research

questions.title: Design a Notification Optimization System
questions.description: Design an ML system that decides which notifications to send, when, and through which channel to maximize user engagement without causing fatigue.
key_discussion_points: Long-term user retention vs short-term engagement
Causal inference for measuring true impact

expected_depth_by_level.E4: Classification for send/don't-send, basic features, engagement metrics
expected_depth_by_level.E5: Multi-objective optimization, causal inference, long-term value modeling, reinforcement learning formulation
